reasoning: Charm is present — pink/red faceted pixel gems with warm tones and a toy-like quality. At 4x on the light bg the color palette reads as ruby/gem and has friendliness. However the silhouette fails the sticker test: at 1x the sprite dissolves into ambiguous pink specks with no readable gem shape. The scattered white highlight pixels that ring each gem at 4x break the edge boundary and prevent a crisp outline, producing a mushy cloud instead of a clean faceted silhouette. On the dark bg some of the deeper red facet pixels merge with black, further eroding the edge. Contrast on light bg is the strongest panel. Overall the charm ingredient is there but clarity ingredient is too weak — charming-but-mushy.
